name: Black Death Reaches Europe
summary: The Black Death arrived in Europe when Genoese ships docked in Messina, bringing a devastating pandemic that would kill millions across the continent.

name: Battle of Crécy
summary: Although fought in 1346, its aftermath shaped 1347 as English forces under Edward III continued their campaign in France during the Hundred Years' War.

name: Siege of Calais Continues
summary: English forces maintained a prolonged siege of Calais, which would end in 1347 with the city's surrender, giving England a strategic foothold in France.

name: Surrender of Calais to England
summary: After a year-long siege, Calais surrendered to Edward III of England, becoming a key English stronghold on the European mainland.

name: Marinid Expansion in North Africa
summary: The Marinid Sultanate expanded its influence in North Africa, consolidating control over key cities and trade routes in the Maghreb.

name: Yuan Dynasty Faces Growing Rebellions
summary: Internal unrest and peasant uprisings intensified during the Yuan Dynasty, weakening Mongol rule and foreshadowing its eventual collapse.
